title: Ciphers at War
description: Follow cryptography through the battlefields — from ancient military codes through the World Wars to Cold War espionage.

stops.label: Polybius Square
stops.note: Ancient Greek military signaling — fire-torch telegraphy across distances.

stops.label: Stager Cipher
stops.note: Union Army route transposition — the cipher that won the telegraph war.

stops.label: Confederate Vigenère
stops.note: The South's brass cipher disk — broken repeatedly by Union codebreakers.

stops.label: ADFGX
stops.note: WWI German field cipher — designed for Morse telegraph, broken by Painvin.

stops.label: ADFGVX
stops.note: Extended version with digits — used in the 1918 Spring Offensive.

stops.label: Enigma
stops.note: The machine that shaped WWII — and the codebreakers who defeated it.

stops.label: Lorenz SZ40/42
stops.note: Hitler's strategic cipher — broken without ever seeing the machine.

stops.label: Nihilist Cipher
stops.note: Russian revolutionary and Cold War spy cipher — Polybius-based encryption.

stops.label: VIC Cipher
stops.note: Cold War masterpiece — the most complex hand cipher ever deployed by a spy.
